description_long: This is a vintage, industrial-style mixer with a tall, cylindrical body mounted on a sturdy, three-legged metal stand. The upper section features a prominent circular handwheel and a vertical shaft, likely used for manual operation or adjustment. Below the handwheel, there is a bowl-shaped mixing chamber with a spout and a side-mounted lever or handle, suggesting a mechanism for dispensing or controlling the contents. The mixer is predominantly painted in an off-white or beige color, with some areas showing signs of wear and rust, especially on the legs. The legs are splayed outward for stability and appear to be made of heavy metal, possibly cast iron or steel. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed mechanical components and a robust, weathered appearance suitable for outdoor or industrial urban environments.
description: A tall, vintage industrial mixer with a cylindrical body, handwheel, and bowl-shaped chamber on a three-legged metal stand, painted off-white with visible wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular handwheel, central shaft, and bowl-shaped mixing chamber with a spout and handle, all mounted on three splayed legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical shaft, handwheel profile, and the protruding spout and lever, with the legs and base structure clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the handwheel, the shaft, and the mixing chamber, with the legs and base forming a stable tripod.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the mechanical components, the side of the mixing chamber, and the full structure of the legs and base.
attribute_control_panel_type: manual handwheel and lever controls, no digital or analog display
attribute_chassis_design: open frame with three splayed metal legs and exposed mechanical components
